process_job¶

Job processing and monitoring: convergence checks, error detection, Slurm queue queries (squeue/sacct), and job submission orchestration.

class automagician.process_job.SacctRecord(job_id, state, exit_code, derived_exit_code, failed_node, max_rss, elapsed)[source]¶

Bases: object

Accounting information for a completed Slurm job, as returned by sacct.

job_id: str¶
state: str¶
exit_code: str¶
derived_exit_code: str¶
failed_node: str¶
max_rss: str¶
elapsed: str¶
automagician.process_job.process_opt(job_directory, machine, opt_jobs, clear_certificate, home_dir, ssh_config, preliminary_results, continue_past_limit, limit, sub_queue, hit_limit)[source]¶

Processes an opt job, checking to see if it has the required files, and is running

If the job is running writes to preliminary results

Updates machine last on for the particular OptJob in opt_jobs

Parameters:

  • job_directory (str) – The directory the job can be found on

  • machine (Machine) – The machine that the user is currently logged on into

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – A set of every opt_job known

  • clear_certificate (bool) – If true, will remove a convergence certificate if it exists for this job, and will calculate its convergence normally

  • home_dir (str) – The home directory of the job

  • ssh_config (SSHConfig) – A config to remote into other machines

  • preliminary_results (TextIO) – A file open that currently has write permissions new results will be writen to this

  • continue_past_limit (bool) – Determines if hitting the limit will raise a JobLimitError, or not

  • limit (int) – How many jobs can currently be submitted at 1 time

  • sub_queue (List[str]) – A list of all jobs to be sibmitted

  • hit_limit (bool) – If the limit has already been set

Throws:

JobLimitError: If the job limit was hit, and continue_past_limit is not set

automagician.process_job.get_residueSFE(job_directory)[source]¶

Currently returns a tuple of 3 zeroes

Goal seems to be to return the step, force, and total energy of the final step in a tuple

automagician.process_job.check_error(job_directory)[source]¶

Returns True if this job reported an error, false otherwise

Parameters:

job_directory (str) – The directory the job can be found on

Returns:

True iff ll_out shows an error, false otherwise

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.determine_convergence(job_directory)[source]¶

Returns if this job has converged, works for all jobs including bulk relaxation.

Creates a fe.dat if CONTCAR and ll_out both exist.

Parameters:

job_directory (str) – A path to the job directory. NO TRAILING SLASHES

Returns:

bool –

True if the job was converged, False otherise

NOTE: if a convergence certificate exists this always returns True This also uses the correct test of convergence for box convergence

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.determine_box_convergence(job_directory)[source]¶

Returns true if box relaxation completed, false otherwise

Parameters:

job_directory (str) – The directory of the job with the box convergence run

Returns:

bool – True iff there is exactly 1 line in the box relaxation

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.is_isif3(job_directory)[source]¶

Returns if the INCAR present in job_directory has the tag ISIF = 3

Parameters:

job_directory (str) – The directory with the potential box relaxion job

Returns:

True if the INCAR has ISIF = 3 (whitespace ignored), false otherwise

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.grep_ll_out_convergence(ll_out)[source]¶

Looks at ll_out to see if the reuqired accuracy has been met

Parameters:

ll_out (str) – A path to ll.out to check accuracy

Returns:

bool – True iff the energy minimization was stopped due to required accuracy being met False otherwise

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.process_converged(job_directory, opt_jobs)[source]¶

creates a convergence certificate, and sets the job status to converged

This would combine XCATCAR and FE if that was working

Parameters:

  • job_directory (str) – A path to the job directory with the converged optomization job.

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – A collection of every optomization job

automagician.process_job.process_unconverged(job_directory, opt_jobs, continue_past_limit, limit, sub_queue, machine, hit_limit, preliminary_results)[source]¶

Adds the final values of the job to the preliminary_results file then resbumits

If CONTCAR does not exist, or is of size 0, only resubmits, same if OUTCAR

does not exist.

Parameters:

  • job_directory (str) – A path to the directory with the unconverged job.

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – A collection of every optomization job known.

  • continue_past_limit (bool) – Setting to true prevents a JobLimitError being raised when hitting the limit.

  • limit (int) – How many jobs to sumit at once.

  • sub_queue (List[str]) – A list of jobs to be submitted.

  • machine (Machine) – The machine that the user is currently on.

  • hit_limit (bool) – If the limit has been hit.

  • preliminary_results (TextIO) – A openend file that is writable, used to note down preliminary results.

Throws:
JobLimitError: if submitting this job would hit the limit, and

continue_past_limit is not set.

automagician.process_job.process_dos(job_directory, opt_jobs, dos_jobs, continue_past_limit, limit, sub_queue, machine, hit_limit)[source]¶

Processes a DOS job and updates its status fields.

If SC or DOS is complete, the corresponding status is set to JobStatus.CONVERGED. If there is an error, the status is set to JobStatus.ERROR.

If the SC directory has an error, dos_status is set to JobStatus.INCOMPLETE. If the DOS directory does not exist yet, it is created and the status is set to JobStatus.RUNNING. If no SC directory exists, one is created and sc_status is set to JobStatus.RUNNING.

Parameters:

  • job_directory (str) – the path to the directory the job is located in

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – A collection of all optimisation jobs known by automagician.

  • dos_jobs (Dict[str, DosJob]) – A collection of all DOS jobs known by automagician.

  • continue_past_limit (bool) – When True, does not raise on hitting the limit.

  • limit (int) – Maximum number of jobs that may be queued at once.

  • sub_queue (List[str]) – Accumulator list of directories pending submission.

  • machine (Machine) – The machine the user is currently logged into.

  • hit_limit (bool) – Whether the submission limit has already been reached.

Changes:

automagician.process_job.process_wav(job_directory, opt_jobs, wav_jobs, continue_past_limit, limit, sub_queue, machine, hit_limit)[source]¶

Processes a wav job, creating the wav directory and submitting if needed.

Waits for the parent optimisation job to converge before acting. If the wav/ subdirectory already exists, checks whether the WAVECAR calculation is complete (JobStatus.CONVERGED) or errored (JobStatus.ERROR). If the directory does not exist yet, creates it via create_wav() and marks the job as JobStatus.RUNNING.

Parameters:

  • job_directory (str) – Path to the optimisation job directory.

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – All known optimisation jobs.

  • wav_jobs (Dict[str, WavJob]) – All known wav jobs.

  • continue_past_limit (bool) – When True, submission continues even after the job limit is reached instead of raising JobLimitError.

  • limit (int) – Maximum number of jobs that may be queued at once.

  • sub_queue (List[str]) – Accumulator list of directories pending submission.

  • machine (Machine) – The machine the user is currently logged into.

  • hit_limit (bool) – Whether the submission limit has already been reached.

automagician.process_job.query_sacct(dirs)[source]¶

Queries sacct for recent jobs matching the given working directories.

Runs sacct for the current user over the past 7 days and matches records by working directory. For directories with multiple recent jobs the last-seen record wins (sacct returns records in submission order).

Uses -X (--allocations) so each job produces exactly one row. MaxRSS is tracked at the step level and will be empty in this output; the field is included for forward-compatibility if that changes.

Parameters:

dirs (List[str]) – Working directory paths to look up in the accounting database.

Returns:

Dict mapping working directory to its most recent SacctRecord. Directories not found in sacct and systems without sacct are omitted.

Return type:

Dict[str, SacctRecord]

automagician.process_job.get_submitted_jobs(machine, opt_jobs, dos_jobs, wav_jobs, tacc_queue_sizes)[source]¶

Ensures only jobs that are actually running have JobStatus.Running set

Every job that has JobStatus.Running set is reset to JobStatus.Incomplete

Then we look at the list of jobs in the queue by the user.

If a job is found and does not exist in opt_jobs, dos_jobs, or wav_jobs then said job is added to the appropate collection with the JobStatus fields set to either Running or Error depending on if the job is has detected an error.

If a job exists in opt_jobs, dos_jobs, or wav_jobs then said jobs status is updated to either JobStatus.Running or JobStatus.Error depending on if the job reported an error.

If a job reported an error the job is cancelled, and the error is logged so the user can look into the error

Parameters:

  • machine (Machine) – The machine the user is currenty logged into

  • opt_jobs (Dict[str, OptJob]) – The collection of all opt jobs known by automagican

  • dos_jobs (Dict[str, DosJob]) – The collection of all dos jobs known by automagican

  • wav_jobs (Dict[str, WavJob]) – The collection of all wav jobs known by automagican

  • tacc_queue_sizes (List[int]) – Shows howmany jobs this user has submitted to TACC

automagician.process_job.gone_job_check(database, opt_jobs)[source]¶

Checks optomization jobs and turns them into gone jobs if they do not exist

A gone job is a job that’s directory is not found

Updates the database of gone_jobs with these new jobs Deletes the gone jobs from the opt_jobs table

automagician.process_job.check_has_opt(job_path, subfile)[source]¶

Checks if the path provides the necessary files for an optimization job :param job_path: a path-like object where the job is located. :type job_path: str :param subfile: the name of the subfile. :type subfile: str

Returns:

bool – True if the list contains all of POSCAR, POTCAR, INCAR, KPOINTS, and the subfile.

Return type:

bool

automagician.process_job.submit_queue(machine, balance, ssh_config, sub_queue, home, tacc_queue_sizes, opt_jobs, dos_jobs, wav_jobs, database, limit)[source]¶

Submits the jobs to the queue of the machine

When submitting to fri-halifax attempts to balance files based on how many jobs are in the queue

When submitting to tacc tires to determine if it will hit the limit then submits the jobs

automagician.process_job.add_to_insta_submit(job_dir, machine, database)[source]¶

Adds the jobs in job_dir into insta_submit

Does not commit changes to the DB
